perturbation.original_text: The parties agree to modify the Agreement as follows: 1. EXTENSION OF TERM: The first paragraph of Section 4 of the Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008."
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree to modify the Agreement as follows: 1. EXTENSION OF TERM: The first paragraph of Section 4 of the Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term Duration"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008."
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Term' to 'Term Duration' introduces confusion as to whether 'Term' refers to the initially defined period or if any other usages of 'Term' still refers to the initial agreement duration. The contradiction arises because the document continues to use the word 'Term' without clarifying if it refers to 'Term Duration'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree to modify the Agreement as follows: 1. EXTENSION OF TERM: The first paragraph of Section 4 of the Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term Duration"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008."
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Term' to 'Term Duration' in the definition of the agreement's duration creates a contradiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, which previously used the term 'Term' to define the expiration date. Now that 'Term Duration' is used instead, any other instances of 'Term' within the agreement become ambiguous. Because the document continues to use 'Term' after redefining the period as 'Term Duration,' it's unclear whether 'Term' refers to the initially defined duration or something else, leading to potential disputes over contract obligations and creating an in-text contradiction as per the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: The notice information for Licensee in Section 17(a) of the Agreement shall be amended such that "Paul Zuzelo" is deleted and replaced with "General Counsel", and such that the email address for Paul Zuzelo is deleted.
perturbation.changed_text: The notice information for Licensee in Section 17(a) of the Agreement shall be amended such that "Paul Zuzelo" is deleted and replaced with "Legal Department", and such that the email address for Paul Zuzelo is deleted.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'General Counsel' to 'Legal Department' introduces ambiguity. It's unclear whether 'Legal Department' encompasses the responsibilities previously assigned to the 'General Counsel,' creating uncertainty in who should be contacted for legal notices and if 'General Counsel' still exists in the company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NOTICE PROVISION: The notice information for Licensee in Section 17(a) of the Agreement shall be amended such that "Paul Zuzelo" is deleted and replaced with "Legal Department", and such that the email address for Paul Zuzelo is deleted.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'General Counsel' to 'Legal Department' in the notice provision does not create a strong contradiction. While it introduces a slight ambiguity, as noted in the explanation, both terms refer to the legal function within the Licensee's organization. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, thus not creating a conflict. A reasonable person could assume that 'Legal Department' encompasses the functions of the 'General Counsel.' This doesn't break compatibility and isn't a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Except as herein expressly amended or by necessary implication modified by this Amendment, the Agreement in all other respects is hereby ratified and shall continue in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as herein expressly amended or by necessary implication modified by this Amendment, the Contract in all other respects is hereby ratified and shall continue in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' after specifically defining 'Agreement' earlier creates confusion. It's now uncertain whether 'Contract' refers to the originally defined 'Agreement' or to some other contractual document. If Agreement is a specific and defined term, the use of 'Contract' creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as herein expressly amended or by necessary implication modified by this Amendment, the Contract in all other respects is hereby ratified and shall continue in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces the defined term 'Agreement' with 'Contract'. The Perturbation Explanation states that doing so creates uncertainty because the 'Agreement' was specifically defined earlier in the document. Because the Contradicted Text uses the term 'Contract', the change makes it unclear whether 'Contract' refers to the 'Agreement' that was already defined, or to some other contract. Therefore, the use of 'Contract' in the Changed Text contradicts the fact that 'Agreement' was the original defined term and directly impacts the meaning of the Contradicted Text, creating a strong in-text contradiction as described in the Misaligned Terminology definition.
